hub_subtitle: Medical English to welcome guests and present with confidence

lesson.menu_desc: Look back at everything: the simple past (was/were, -ed), polite requests (Can you/Could you), connectors (and/but/because), and writing a short email. A calm meeting, a self-assessment journal, and a confidence check. -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 15 -- Review & Confidence Check
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Graziele Dias | Aula 15 -- Review & Confidence Check

listenings.text: Hello Graziele. Great work in the last few weeks. You learned a lot. You can write a short email, you can talk about the past, and you can ask for help politely. Today we review everything together. Stay calm, take your time, and show me what you can do. I am sure you are ready.

Hi Graziele, it is Ana. A quick tip before your review. Remember three things. For the past, add e d: worked, studied, joined. For a polite request, use Can you or Could you and the base verb. And for an email, start with a greeting and finish with Best regards or Sincerely. You know all of this. Be confident!

extra_audio.text: Graziele writes a short email after the meeting. First, the greeting: Hello Doctor Lewis. Then, the past: the meeting was great. Next, a polite request: Could you send the agenda? After that, a thank you: thank you, and have a good day. Finally, the closing: Best regards, Graziele.
